name: Classic Asterley Negroni
description: The quintessential aperitivo cocktail made with our Estate sweet vermouth and Asterley Original for a uniquely British take.
method: Add all ingredients to a rocks glass filled with ice.
Stir gently for 15-20 seconds to chill and dilute.
Garnish with an orange peel, expressing the oils over the drink.

name: Asterley Spritz
description: A refreshing, effervescent aperitif — our take on the Italian Spritz using Asterley Original.
method: Fill a large wine glass with ice.
Pour in the Asterley Original, then the sparkling wine.
Top with a splash of soda water.
Stir gently and garnish with an orange slice.

name: Perfect Dry Martini
description: An elegant, classic Martini showcasing Schofield's dry vermouth with its aromatic white flower character.
method: Add gin, Schofield's, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with ice.
Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled.
Strain into a chilled coupe or Martini glass.
Garnish with a lemon twist or olive.

name: Wet Martini
description: A more balanced, vermouth-forward Martini for those who enjoy a softer, more aromatic cocktail.
method: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 20-30 seconds.
Strain into a chilled Martini glass.
Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: Manhattan
description: A rich, warming cocktail made with Estate sweet vermouth and rye whisky — perfect for autumn and winter evenings.
method: Add all ingredients to a mixing glass filled with ice.
Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled.
Strain into a chilled coupe glass.
Garnish with a brandied cherry.

name: Paper Plane
description: A modern classic with equal parts whisky, Asterley Original, amaro, and lemon. Perfectly balanced and endlessly drinkable.
method: Add all ingredients to a cocktail shaker with ice.
Shake vigorously for 10-15 seconds.
Double strain into a chilled coupe glass.
No garnish needed — let the flavours speak.

name: Wimbledon Cup
description: A quintessentially British summer serve using Dispense amaro — refreshing, herbal, and perfect for garden parties.
method: Add Dispense and gin to a tall glass filled with ice.
Top with ginger ale.
Garnish generously with strawberries, cucumber, and mint.

name: Toronto
description: A bold, spirit-forward cocktail showcasing Britannica fernet with rye whisky. Dark, complex, and deeply satisfying.
method: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled.
Strain into a chilled rocks glass or coupe.
Garnish with an orange peel.

name: Dispense & Ginger Highball
description: The simplest way to enjoy our amaro — long, refreshing, and effortlessly sophisticated.
method: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Pour in the Dispense.
Top with ginger ale and stir gently.
Garnish with an orange slice.

name: The Matriarch
description: A complex, stirred cocktail combining Dispense with whisky and Maraschino — for the adventurous drinker.
method: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled.
Strain into a chilled coupe glass.
Garnish with a lemon twist.

name: Estate & Tonic
description: A beautifully simple long serve — Estate sweet vermouth with premium tonic and citrus. The perfect introduction to vermouth.
method: Fill a large wine glass or highball with ice.
Pour in the Estate vermouth.
Top with tonic water and stir gently.
Garnish with an orange slice.

name: Martinez
description: The predecessor to the Martini — rich, sweet, and aromatic. Estate vermouth shines in this forgotten classic.
method: Add all ingredients to a mixing glass filled with ice.
Stir for 30 seconds until well chilled.
Strain into a chilled coupe glass.
Garnish with an orange twist.
